title: 'Twisted Face,' the French Gestapo collaborator who arrested historian and resistance fighter Marc Bloch
subhead: France André, known as 'Twisted Face,' was a staunch French collaborator who led a gang of nearly 100 people in the Lyon area. They spread terror through thefts, arrests, torture and murders, all committed for personal gain and to serve the Nazis. Among his many victims was Marc Bloch, a Jewish historian and resistance fighter who will be inducted into the Panthéon on June 23.

France hosts annual music street festival despite worsening heatwave
subhead: The Fête de la Musique coincides with a ferocious heatwave, with a record 35 departments – roughly a third of the country – placed under the highest heat alert. The government has banned alcohol consumption in public spaces in departments under red alert.

title: 'Trump remains the only world leader to have recognized Israel's annexation of the Golan Heights'
subhead: The strategic importance of the Golan Heights, annexed by Israel since 1981, has only increased following the overthrow of Bashar al-Assad in 2024.

title: 'Despite the tracksuits, we have brains': The French youth staging literary battles in housing projects
subhead: The 'projects book club' has become a sensation on social media. By pitting Zola against Flaubert and Adam Smith against Karl Marx with humor, 'Geronimo' and his friends aim to bring writers and sociologists to new audiences.

title: Paris's Quai Branly Museum turns 20: A look back at its turbulent history
subhead: The Paris institution is celebrating its anniversary with great fanfare this weekend. But behind the festivities, the museum continues to face many challenges, particularly economic.

Le Monde's point-by-point breakdown of the fragile US-Iran framework agreement
subhead: After more than 100 days of war, the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending their conflict. The document will be followed by negotiations to address issues it does not resolve. Le Monde publishes the full text, along with context and analysis.

title: After US signals another review of its military presence in Europe, allies grapple with Washington's ambiguities
subhead: On June 18, the US defense secretary announced a 'review' of the distribution of American NATO forces on the continent. The decision has raised concerns, even though it is being presented as a simple 'adjustment,' a senior US defense official told Le Monde.

title: Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
subhead: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.
